---
title: "LRPMM01: Correlated-domain-factor measurement model (vocabulary / code / grammar)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
loadings = pd.read_csv(_here / "loadings_summary.csv")
factor_correlation = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_correlation.csv", index_col=0)
structural = pd.read_csv(_here / "structural_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPMM01 is the DAG-sanctioned successor to the closed LRP66. Instead of a single
latent general ability `g`, it estimates **correlated domain factors** -
vocabulary, code, and grammar - from the standardised T1 skill indicators, then
regresses word-reading gain on those latent factors. It recovers the one output no
merged model produces: an explicit **factor-loadings / communality** table and a
partial **measurement-error correction** of the skill slopes.

::: {.callout-important title="Measurement / triangulation, NOT causal"}

This is a **measurement** model. Per the locked DAG's **ID-2**, every factor ->
gain slope is a **latent-ability-confounded adjusted association** - the factor
model improves the measurement match, it does **not** identify effects. The
randomised causal claim lives in the ITT suite (`lrpitt10` for word reading) and
the `gain_factors` randomised term, not here.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Fragility at n ~ 51"}

A correlated-factor latent model at n ~ 51 is fragile and prior-dependent. Read it
as **triangulation** against the `gain_factors` family, with the **wide intervals
stated as the honest result** (exactly as the closed LRP66 did). The
reporting-tier re-fit gives the final magnitudes.

:::

## Model

**Measurement** (correlated unit-variance factors; loadings positive so a loading
is an indicator-factor correlation and $\lambda^2$ is the communality):

$$
\mathbf{f}_i \sim \mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \Omega), \quad \Omega \sim \text{LKJ}(\eta),
\qquad
Z_{ij} \sim \mathcal{N}\!\big(\lambda_j\, f_{i,\,d(j)},\ \sigma_j\big),
$$

**Structural** (Beta-Binomial reading-gain leg on the latent factors):

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}_*(W_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
        + \sum_d \beta_d\, f_{i,d}
        + \beta_{\text{blocks}}\,\text{blocks}_i + \beta_A\,A_{\text{std},i}.
$$

Domains (core set): **vocabulary** {R, E}, **code** {L, B}, **grammar** {F, T}.
Speech is unavailable as a standardised indicator here and is omitted; non-verbal
MA (`blocks`) enters the structural leg as an observed covariate, not a factor.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.png){width=32%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.png){width=32%}
![predictor slope](prior_predictor_slope.png){width=32%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.png){width=32%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Loadings and communalities

```{python}
# | echo: false
loadings
```

- `loading_*`: the indicator-factor loading (a correlation, since factors and
  indicators are standardised; mean + equal-tailed central interval).
- `communality_*`: $\lambda^2 / (\lambda^2 + \sigma^2)$ - the share of that
  indicator explained by its domain factor.

## Factor correlation matrix

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_correlation
```

Posterior-mean correlations among the latent domain factors (LKJ prior). High
off-diagonal correlations are the quantitative form of the "positive manifold" that
made a single `g` tempting; reporting them explicitly is the point of the move to
correlated domain factors.

## Structural slopes (factor -> reading gain)

```{python}
# | echo: false
structural
```

Each `beta_<domain>` is the measurement-error-corrected slope of word-reading gain
on that latent factor, plus the observed `beta_age` / `beta_blocks` adjusters. Per
ID-2 these are **adjusted associations**, not causal effects; `prob_pos` is the
posterior probability the slope is positive.

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
